{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the common abnormalities seen in a fetus with congenital CMV infection. Reviewing the provided documents, Document [7] (Title: Congenital Cytomegalovirus Infection -- History and Physical) mentions that congenital CMV infection can result in various fetal abnormalities, including microcephaly, intracranial calcifications, ventriculomegaly, lenticulostriate vasculopathy, echogenic bowel, intrauterine growth restriction, hepatomegaly, pericardial effusion, ascites, placental inflammation, or fetal death. Among the given options, pulmonary cyst is not listed as a common abnormality. However, myocarditis is mentioned as a potential complication of congenital infections, including congenital CMV. Therefore, the most appropriate answer is B: Myocarditis.", "answer_choice": "B"}
